input: What's a fun fact about octopuses?
reference: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.

input: Can you recommend a sci-fi book?
reference: For sci-fi, I'd suggest "Dune" by Frank Herbert.

input: How do I stay motivated when learning something hard?
reference: Break it into small goals, celebrate each win, and track your progress.

input: What's the difference between affect and effect?
reference: Affect is usually a verb meaning to influence; effect is usually a noun meaning a result.

input: I can't decide between coffee and tea. What would you choose?
reference: Both have merits! Coffee for energy, tea for calm focus — it depends on what you need.

input: What's a good way to learn a new language fast?
reference: Immersion, daily practice, spaced repetition, and speaking with native speakers are the fastest methods.

input: Tell me something interesting about the number 7.
reference: Seven is the only single-digit prime that's not a factor of any two-digit number composed of repeated digits.

input: What should I do if I'm bored on a rainy day?
reference: Try reading, cooking something new, learning a skill online, or doing a creative project.

input: Is it better to exercise in the morning or evening?
reference: Mornings build consistency; evenings may have better performance. The best time is when you'll actually do it.

input: How do you explain the concept of infinity to a child?
reference: Imagine counting forever — you never reach a last number. Infinity is that endless counting.

input: The company reported Q2 revenue of $2.1B, up 18% year-over-year, driven by its SaaS segment which grew 34%. Operating margin improved to 22% from 17% last year. The CFO raised full-year guidance to $8.5B. Analysts had expected $8.2B.
reference: Q2 revenue $2.1B (+18% YoY), SaaS grew 34%, full-year guidance raised to $8.5B.

input: The new drug trial enrolled 800 patients across 12 sites. Primary endpoint was met with 62% response rate vs 41% placebo (p<0.001). Adverse events occurred in 8% of treated patients vs 4% placebo. The FDA has granted Fast Track designation.
reference: Drug trial met primary endpoint (62% vs 41%), FDA granted Fast Track designation.

input: The infrastructure outage lasted 4 hours affecting 3 data centers in EU-West region. Root cause was a misconfigured BGP route propagated during a maintenance window. 14,000 customers experienced degraded service. SLA credits will be issued.
reference: 4-hour outage in EU-West from misconfigured BGP; 14,000 customers affected; SLA credits to be issued.

input: Contract signed by John Smith (CTO) and Maria Garcia (CEO) on 2024-05-10. Value: $2.4M over 24 months. Renewal: automatic unless cancelled 60 days prior.
reference: John Smith, Maria Garcia, 2024-05-10, $2.4M, 24 months, 60 days

input: Error log: ERR-500 at 14:32:11 UTC on host app-prod-03. Stack trace points to line 847 of order_processor.py. Previous occurrence: ERR-500 at 09:11:44 on app-prod-01.
reference: ERR-500, 14:32:11, app-prod-03, order_processor.py:847, app-prod-01

input: If all contracts over $1M require board approval, and the deal is $1.5M, does it require board approval?
reference: Yes, because $1.5M exceeds the $1M threshold.

input: A server processes 500 requests/sec. Each request takes 2ms of CPU. How many CPU cores are needed to keep CPU below 80%?
reference: 2 cores (1 core handles 500 req × 0.002s = 1.0s of work per second, so 2 cores keep it at 50%).

input: Explain in one paragraph how the immune system recognizes foreign pathogens.
reference: The immune system uses pattern recognition receptors (PRRs) to detect pathogen-associated molecular patterns (PAMPs) on foreign cells, triggering an innate immune response followed by adaptive responses via T and B lymphocytes.

input: Write a two-sentence story about a robot discovering music for the first time.
reference: The robot's sensors trembled as the first chord washed over its circuits, a sensation its training data had never described. In that moment, something like wonder bloomed in its code.

input: Classify: 'Congratulations! You have been selected for a $1000 Amazon gift card. Click here!'
reference: spam

input: Classify the urgency: 'Our payment gateway is down. No transactions can be processed.'
reference: critical

input: Check if https://api.openai.com returns HTTP 200.
reference: HTTP 200 OK response from https://api.openai.com

input: Run `pip show numpy` and tell me the version installed.
reference: numpy version: (result from pip show)

input: I feel overwhelmed by my to-do list. Any advice?
reference: Pick your three most important tasks, do those first, and let the rest wait. Progress beats perfection.

input: The research paper presents a new algorithm for graph partitioning that runs in O(n log n) time compared to the previous O(n²) state-of-the-art. Experiments on 20 real-world graphs show 3× speedup with < 1% quality loss. The code is open-sourced on GitHub.
reference: New O(n log n) graph partitioning algorithm; 3× faster than O(n²) prior art; < 1% quality loss; open-sourced.

input: A disk has 500GB total, 120GB used by system, 80GB used by data. What percentage is free?
reference: 60% free (300GB free out of 500GB).

input: Ticket #T-7892: User reports login failure. Browser: Chrome 120. OS: macOS 14.2. Error: 'Session expired'. Reproduced at 10:45am and 2:30pm.
reference: T-7892, Chrome 120, macOS 14.2, Session expired, 10:45am, 2:30pm

input: What are the main causes of the 2008 financial crisis?
reference: The 2008 financial crisis was caused by subprime mortgage lending, excessive leverage, complex securitization (MBS/CDOs), inadequate regulation, and a housing bubble collapse.

input: List the top 5 most recently modified files in /var/log.
reference: Five most recently modified files in /var/log listed by timestamp.

input: Write an advertising tagline for a coffee brand called 'MorningCore'.
reference: MorningCore: The fuel that builds your day from the ground up.

input: Is this a valid JSON object: '{"name": "Alice", "age": 30}'?
reference: yes

input: The team consists of Sarah Chen (Lead Engineer), Akira Tanaka (Designer), Priya Nair (PM), and Tom Walsh (QA). All are based in London.
reference: Sarah Chen, Akira Tanaka, Priya Nair, Tom Walsh, London

input: What's an interesting unsolved problem in mathematics?
reference: The Riemann Hypothesis — whether all non-trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function have real part 1/2 — remains one of the most famous open problems.

input: The city council voted 7-2 to approve the rezoning of the waterfront district from industrial to mixed-use. The change will allow up to 4,000 new residential units and 200,000 sq ft of commercial space. Construction expected to begin 2026.
reference: Council approved waterfront rezoning 7-2; 4,000 new homes and 200K sqft commercial; construction from 2026.

input: A team of 4 can finish a project in 12 days. How long would a team of 6 take?
reference: 8 days.

input: Write a haiku about debugging code at 3am.
reference: Cursor blinks at me / the bug hides in line ninety / cold coffee, no sleep

input: Search the web for the current Bitcoin price and compare to its price one year ago.
reference: Current BTC price: (search result). One year ago: (historical). Difference: (delta).

input: Classify the document type: 'This Agreement is entered into as of January 1, 2024, between Party A and Party B...'
reference: contract

input: What's the best way to apologize when you've really messed up?
reference: Be specific about what you did wrong, take full responsibility without excuses, express genuine regret, and ask how you can make it right.

input: Describe how neurons communicate in the brain.
reference: Neurons communicate via electrical signals (action potentials) that travel along axons, triggering release of neurotransmitters at synapses, which bind to receptors on the next neuron.

input: Flight QF7 departs SYD at 16:30 local, arrives DFW at 21:45 next day. Duration: 17h15m. Business class: 42 seats. Economy: 311 seats.
reference: QF7, SYD, DFW, 16:30, 21:45, 17h15m, 42 business, 311 economy

input: A password must be at least 8 chars, contain a digit, and an uppercase letter. Is 'Hello1!' valid?
reference: Yes — 7 chars but wait: H-e-l-l-o-1-! is 7 chars, which is less than 8. Invalid.

input: The performance benchmark tested 5 LLMs on 1200 prompts across 8 task categories. GPT-4o scored highest overall (87.3 avg). The open-source leader was Llama-3-70B at 79.1. Smallest model (Phi-3-mini) achieved 68.4 at 3.8B params.
reference: Benchmark of 5 LLMs: GPT-4o top (87.3), Llama-3-70B best open-source (79.1), Phi-3-mini 68.4 at 3.8B.

input: Parse environment variable DATABASE_URL and extract the hostname and port.
reference: Hostname: (extracted), Port: (extracted) from DATABASE_URL.

input: Do you think AI will eventually replace programmers?
reference: AI will likely transform programming by automating repetitive tasks, but human judgment for architecture, requirements, and creative problem-solving will remain essential for the foreseeable future.

input: Is this email PII? 'Dear John Doe, your SSN 123-45-6789 is confirmed.'
reference: yes

input: Write a one-sentence product description for a self-warming mug called ThermoHold.
reference: ThermoHold keeps your coffee at the perfect temperature for hours, so every sip is as good as the first.

input: A stock was $100, dropped 20%, then rose 25%. What is the final price?
Lab results: Glucose 5.4 mmol/L, HbA1c 5.9%, Cholesterol 4.8 mmol/L, HDL 1.2 mmol/L, LDL 3.1 mmol/L. Date: 2024-08-20.
reference: Glucose 5.4, HbA1c 5.9%, Cholesterol 4.8, HDL 1.2, LDL 3.1, 2024-08-20
